name: Hotcakes
recipeInstructions:
1) In a large bowl, stir together the flour, sugar, and baking powder until combined. In another bowl, beat the milk, melted butter, egg, vanilla, and food coloring until well mixed. Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until just blended (leaving a few lumps is preferable to overmixing and resulting in tough hotcakes). For lighter hotcakes, allow the batter to rest at room temperature for about 30 minutes or refrigerate it up to one day.
2) Heat a griddle or large heavy pan (cast iron works well) over medium heat. The griddle is ready when a drop of water sizzles and quickly evaporates on the surface. Lightly brush the griddle with melted butter. Pour about 1/3 cup batter for each hotcake, leaving space between them to facilitate turning. Cook until the bottoms are golden brown (check by lifting a corner) and bubbles form on the topsides. Gently flip the hotcakes and cook until the undersides are golden brown. Repeat with the remaining batter. Serve warm with butter and maple syrup.
